description_long: This is a tall, cylindrical wooden pole with a rough, natural texture and an uneven, partially bark-covered surface. One side of the pole features an intricately carved owl in relief, with detailed feathers, facial features, and wings, giving it a decorative and artistic appearance. The rest of the pole retains its raw, unfinished wood look, with visible grain, cracks, and some weathering. The top and bottom surfaces are flat, indicating it may have been cut from a larger log. The pole appears solid and heavy, likely intended as a decorative or commemorative feature in a park or urban green space. There are no visible attachments such as signs, lights, or cables, and the base suggests it would be embedded in the ground for stability.
description: A tall, cylindrical wooden pole with a detailed owl carving on one side and rough, natural wood texture on the rest. The pole is solid and decorative, likely used as an artistic feature in urban parks.
description_view_0: Shows the plain, rough side of the pole with minimal detail and no carvings visible.
description_view_1: Displays the edge of the pole, revealing the transition from the rough wood to the carved owl relief.
description_view_2: Presents the front with the detailed owl carving, highlighting the artistic and decorative aspect.
description_view_3: Shows the side profile of the owl carving, with the wing and body contours visible against the wood.
